response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a technically broad individual contributor with a strong full-stack background, but the profile does not come close to meeting the requirements for a CTO role. There is no engineering leadership or management experience of any kind — no evidence of hiring, team scaling, or owning architectural domains at an organizational level. Agentic AI experience is limited to roughly one month at the current employer, with no production multi-agent systems, LLM infrastructure, evaluation pipelines, or regulated-environment AI deployments in evidence. The fintech exposure at RIDE Capital is real but developer-level, not architectural or executive in scope.
strengths: Broad polyglot technical foundation across backend, frontend, mobile, and embedded systems, demonstrating adaptability across problem domains
Hands-on distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, event-driven microservices
Early exposure to fintech engineering practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) at RIDE Capital in a regulated German investment context
concerns: Zero evidence of engineering leadership — no team management, hiring, mentoring, or organizational scaling at any point in a 20-year career
Agentic AI experience is limited to one month at current role; no production multi-agent systems, LLM selection, evaluation methodology, or AI observability infrastructure
Career trajectory is Staff/Senior Software Developer throughout — no CTO, VP Engineering, or any executive or director-level role that would indicate readiness for this position

key_factors.factor: No engineering leadership or team-scaling experience
Distributed systems and high-throughput backend background
